sweepga_help_head:
Efficient pangenome alignment filtering and sparsification tool

Usage: sweepga [OPTIONS] [FILE]...

Arguments:
  [FILE]...
          Input files: FASTA (1+) or PAF (1 only), auto-detected
          
            1 FASTA: align to self and filter
            2+ FASTA: align all pairs and filter
            1 PAF: filter alignments
            stdin: auto-detect and process

Options:
      --output-file <OUTPUT_FILE>
          Output file path (auto-detects format from extension: .paf or .1aln)

      --paf
          Output PAF format (default, kept for clarity and backwards compatibility)

      --1aln
          Output .1aln binary format instead of default PAF

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

  -V, --version
          Print version

Alignment options:
      --aligner <ALIGNER>
          Aligner to use for FASTA input
          
          [default: fastga]
          [possible values: fastga, wfmash]

      --fastga
          Use FastGA aligner (shorthand for --aligner fastga)

      --wfmash
          Use wfmash aligner (shorthand for --aligner wfmash)

      --fastga-frequency <FREQUENCY>
          FastGA k-mer frequency threshold (use k-mers occurring ≤ N times). Overrides --fastga-frequency-multiplier when set

      --fastga-frequency-multiplier <FASTGA_FREQUENCY_MULTIPLIER>
          FastGA k-mer frequency multiplier: when --fastga-frequency is unset, the effective frequency = `num_pansn_haplotypes * multiplier` (clamped to at least 10). Default 1 reproduces FastGA's historical behavior of one k-mer hit per haplotype
          
          [default: 1]

      --map-pct-identity <MAP_PCT_IDENTITY>
          Minimum percent identity for wfmash mapping (e.g. "90" or ANI preset "ani50-2")

      --all-pairs
          Align all genome pairs separately (slower, uses more memory, but handles many genomes)

      --batch-bytes <BATCH_BYTES>
          Maximum sequence data per batch (e.g., "50M", "2G"). Partitions genomes into batches. Stored as a raw string so library consumers (like impg's `sweepga::align_self_paf`) can pass it verbatim; parse via `crate::cli::parse_metric_number` at use sites when you need a numeric byte count

      --batch-size <BATCH_SIZE>
          Explicit number of genomes per batch (manual override). Overrides --batch-bytes and --max-disk

      --max-disk <MAX_DISK>
          Maximum disk space for temporary files during alignment (e.g., "100G", "500M"). Computes batch size automatically to stay within budget. Overrides --batch-bytes

      --zstd
          Compress k-mer index with zstd for ~2x disk savings and faster I/O

      --zstd-level <ZSTD_LEVEL>
          Zstd compression level (1-19, higher = smaller files but slower). Default: 3
          
          [default: 3]

      --temp-dir <TEMPDIR>
          Temporary directory for intermediate files (defaults to TMPDIR env var, then current directory; use "ramdisk" for /dev/shm)

Basic filtering:
      --min-aln-length <BLOCK_LENGTH>
          Minimum alignment block length (omit to use aligner default)

      --num-mappings <NUM_MAPPINGS>
          n:m-best mappings kept in query:target dimensions. 1:1 (orthogonal), use ∞/many for unbounded. Default matches impg's historical pangenome setting: no mapping-axis filter before scaffolding
          
          [default: many:many]

      --overlap <OVERLAP>
          Maximum overlap ratio for plane sweep filtering
          
          [default: 0.95]

      --scoring <SCORING>
          Scoring function for plane sweep
          
          [default: log-length-ani]
          [possible values: ani, length, length-ani, log-length-ani, matches]

      --min-aln-identity <MIN_IDENTITY>
          Minimum per-mapping identity filter applied to PAF records after alignment (0-1 fraction, 1-100%, or "aniN" for Nth percentile). This is a post-alignment PAF filter, not an alignment-stage knob
